skills: React development with hooks and functional components
IndexedDB and localStorage for data persistence
Web manifest v3 for browser extensions

rules.title: Translation Files
rules.description: en_GB.json is the base translation file. Any other translation file should be updated by editing en_GB.json first, then running `bun run translations` to ensure formatting remains consistent across all locales.

rules.title: Branch Strategy
rules.description: Use the three-branch workflow: dev (active development) → beta (release candidates) → main (production/stable). All PRs should target the dev branch.

rules.title: Commit Messages
rules.description: Follow conventional commits format. Use commitlint standards (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, etc.) for all commit messages.

rules.title: Code Style
rules.description: Run `bun run lint:fix` and `bun run pretty` before committing. Follow the ESLint configuration in eslint.config.js.

rules.title: Build Targets
rules.description: The project builds for multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari). Test changes across all targets when modifying core functionality or manifests.

rules.title: Package Manager
rules.description: Always use Bun (not npm or yarn) for dependency management and running scripts. The project requires Bun >= 1.3.0.

rules.title: File Organization
rules.description: Components go in src/components/, features in src/features/, utilities in src/utils/. Keep the existing folder structure consistent.

rules.title: State Management
rules.description: Use localStorage via useLocalStorageState hook for persistent settings. Use React Context for shared state (see src/contexts/).

rules.title: Styling
rules.description: SCSS files are organized in src/scss/. Use the existing variables (_variables.scss) and mixins (_mixins.scss) for consistency.

rules.title: Development Server
rules.description: Use `bun run dev` for local development with hot reload. Use `bun run dev:host` to make it accessible on the network for testing on other devices.
